title: DA hike delayed, arrears expected in April: 5 reasons behind the unusual delay this year
summary: The central government is expected to announce a DA hike in April 2026, benefitting over 1 crore employees and pensioners. &nbsp;The unusual delay is likely due to administrative checks, the 8th Pay Commission transition and the approval process, with arrears assured, say experts.

title: Understanding jewellery insurance: Here's all you need to know about securing your gold against risks
summary: Indian households hold 25,000-30,000 tonnes of gold, valued at nearly $5 trillion. With limited bank locker availability, experts recommend jewellery insurance to cover risks like theft and natural disasters, especially as gold prices rise.

summary: Despite the discipline, your portfolio lacks the diversification and high-growth potential that other asset classes can provide.

title: Small savings offer interest rates up to 8.2% in Q1FY27, but don’t lock your money without these 5 key checks
summary: Small savings rates for the first quarter of FY27 remain stable at up to 8.2%, offering secure returns. Investors should assess goals, tenure, tax benefits, liquidity and interest trends before choosing suitable schemes.

title: The volatility trap: Why straddles and strangles fail when you need them most
summary: Options sold as “volatility plays” hide a structural flaw—one retail traders often discover too late, after the market moves, premiums collapse, and the trade that seemed safe quietly turns costly.

title: Rupee hits ₹95: 6 ways the fall affects your personal finances, experts weigh in
summary: Rupee hits an all-time low against the dollar, impacting personal finances with rising costs on products and services, costlier imports, and higher EMIs. Such a situation requires proper decision-making and long-term investment strategies, highlighting the importance of smart financial planning.

title: Diversification feels boring—until markets fall: What investors are missing
summary: Equity SIPs are delivering negative returns despite disciplined investing, data shared by Zerodha’s Nithin Kamath shows. Investors often overlook diversification in bull markets. Here’s why balancing equity with debt and gold helps manage risk and stay invested through volatility.

title: From UAE to India: When a temporary return triggers lasting tax risks
summary: Returnees from the UAE face shifting residency status, global taxation exposure and compliance burdens as temporary moves risk becoming prolonged stays.

title: FY27 Begins: Post Office TD rates up to 7.5% outpace bank FDs in April–June quarter
summary: Post Office Time Deposit rates for FY27 offer stable, government-backed returns, often higher than bank fixed deposits, making Post Office Time Deposit a reliable choice for conservative investors seeking predictable income.

title: Expenses rise with summer heat: Weather-proof your budget
summary: From soaring electricity bills and AC cab rides to vacations, summer camps and medical buffers, India’s summer can raise household expenses by 10–12%. Here’s how to plan ahead.

title: US Stocks Today | Massive short squeeze fogs market’s view on Iran peace prospects
summary: US stocks staged their biggest rally in months, but much of the surge was driven not by optimism over Iran peace prospects but by a massive short squeeze. Heavily shorted stocks jumped sharply as traders rushed to unwind bearish bets, amplifying gains across major indexes and lifting tech giants.

Institutional investment in real estate down 62 pc in Jan-Mar to USD 1.4 bn from Dec qtr: Vestian
summary: Institutional investment in Indian real estate sector fell 62 per cent during January-March period to USD 1.41 billion from the previous quarter amid negative sentiments because of the West Asia conflict, according to Vestian.

title: West Asia crisis may push up realty construction costs by 5pc in near term
summary: The escalating West Asia conflict is beginning to exert cost pressure on India's real estate sector, with material prices rising and industry leaders warning of a potential 5 per cent spike in construction costs if hostilities persist through April.

title: Mahagun Group raises Rs 225 cr from CSL Finance to complete realty projects
summary: In a statement on Wednesday, the company said it has secured Rs 225 crore funding from CSL Finance. This will strengthen the company's financial position and provide fresh momentum to accelerate project execution across its portfolio.

title: Dollar falls for second day as Middle East ceasefire expectations rise
summary: The dollar dropped for a second day on Wednesday as expectations of a ceasefire in the Middle East ​conflict grew after the U.S. signalled that an end ​to the war could be near, even though markets remained on edge on fears of escalation.

title: Breaking the pattern: A downcycle without macro stress?
summary: India’s latest market downturn is unusual because stock prices have corrected sharply despite a resilient macro backdrop. Unlike past cycles marked by fiscal weakness, high NPAs, or external stress, key indicators remain stable. This divergence suggests the potential for a faster, V-shaped recovery,
